chapters.title: Many factors negatively impact our lives, and we need to identify them to grow
chapters.text:
Addiction is a global issue that calls for immediate action. It requires a collective effort to end it, but unfortunately, it seems like we are not doing enough. Too many things have conditioned us to make it very difficult to ignore; processed foods with additives dominate every supermarket, and the sale of carbonated drinks is at an all-time high. Alcohol is easily accessible to almost everyone, and more people consume cigarettes every day. All these things can have a huge side effect on us in the future if we don't put a stop to it now.

Battling addiction is not an easy task, especially for those who have no idea how to. It's easy to suffer relapses that make us want to give up, but we mustn't. We only have to look forward to better days ahead and remain resolute in our quest to live healthier lives.

In this bite-sized summary, we'll see how the author, Rich Roll, battled alcohol addiction and moved from being overweight to being one of the fittest athletes in the world. Of course, his journey wasn't devoid of setbacks and pain, but his resolve to change for good helped him pull through.

It's never too late to achieve our goals once we set our heart to them.

Temptations and setbacks will always occur, but we can break any addiction once we are committed, as we'll see in the subsequent chapters.

chapters.title: Our lives are generally defined by a few decisive moments — choices that make a huge difference
chapters.text:
We all need a specific moment when we realize that our lives have to change. Moments like these should not be wasted; they should be revered and grasped at all costs because they don’t come around frequently, if at all. Even if we’ve just had one intense experience, we’ve been fortunate because if we blink or turn away for even a second, the door doesn’t just shut; it disappears. Rich Roll’s defining life moment was the period when he’d been given a chance to live a better life. He had labored earlier in his home after climbing only seven steps and knew his body form and size were driving him down the wrong path. Looking in the mirror one night prompted him to change his life. He instinctively knew then he had to take action.

It’s critical to understand ourselves and recognize that any long-term lifestyle change necessitates rigor, precision, and accountability.

Vague ideas like eating better or getting to the gym frequently aren't going to cut it. We need a strict and urgent plan to get in shape. It is also important to establish clear boundaries.

It is crucial to realize that dieting isn’t the same as starvation. Every day, we must ensure that our bodies are fortified with essential nutrients in liquid form. However, we should also try to put our reservations aside and hurl ourselves into the process of being fit.

Our first objective to living a healthier life is to seize that rare opportunity — to step through the open door and take a stand. Next, we’ll need a strategy for expanding on what we’ve already accomplished. This entails achieving some equilibrium between dumping old habits and creating new ones. Finally, we need a solid strategy to move forward since we are afraid of simply relapsing into old habits. It’s not so much a diet as it is a long-term regimen. In reality, we require a new way of existence.

chapters.title: The best way to avoid getting drunk is to stop drinking alcohol completely
chapters.text:
Rich Roll had a nagging feeling that alcohol would be an issue since Bruce Kimball, a close friend, handed him his first beer. He liked it too much; he felt it was a magical cure for his social shortcomings. Despite this insight, he had no intention of acting on it; it was only a foreshadowing of the things to come. As a result, he tucked the idea away. He believed that if he pretended there wasn’t an issue, there wouldn’t be one.

Rich Roll was an alcoholic, and he couldn’t get enough of the alcohol he consumed. He was doing everything he could to succeed on a conscious level. However, in reflection, he saw that his daily torture sessions were an unconscious and compulsive attempt to eliminate the anguish of his shyness. In contrast to the dissociation and emotional numbness that had previously characterized him, drinking made him feel alive.

Alcohol gives us short-term gains by changing the course of our lives and giving us a profound warmth that makes us feel better.

Drinking made all his sensations of dread, hatred, insecurity, and loneliness evaporate in a moment, replaced with a surge of warmth and belonging. His primary focus was to figure out how to drink more.

He felt better the more he drank. He enjoyed what he imagined it must be like to be normal for the first time in his life — to go into a gathering of people and initiate a spontaneous discourse, to look someone in the eye and make a joke, to flirt with a lady, laugh, and feel happy within himself. He began to engage in amusing discussions. He knew alcohol would pose a problem for him at that time, but he didn't stop drinking because he felt he could control it.

chapters.title: Alcohol distorts judgments and makes rational thinking difficult
chapters.text:
He fell in love with Michele, a Palo Alto resident, in the spring of 1995. They met in a pub, where he had returned to drink. He was also, predictably, intoxicated. His excessive drinking defined their relationship, which had many highs and lows.

The problem with alcohol is that it provides a temporary feeling of well-being. It’s a quick fix for a huge problem, but the trouble is that we have to drink more to retain the sense of relief it provides. More drinking becomes a problem itself, and we’ll reach a point where we can’t be happy until we drink. Alcoholism thrives on minor, often preventable errors.

Pursue what’s in your heart, and the universe will conspire to support you. ~ Rich Roll

If someone is pulled over after a DUI in most developed countries, the presumption is that the vehicle is stolen, the individual has a warrant out for their arrest, or a weapon hidden beneath the seat. And, unless shown differently, the individual is considered a suspect. This implies that drinking alcohol leads us to become paranoid and restless, as restlessness is the main reason why the police become suspicious of an individual. As a result, drinking is not the answer to our issues but rather the cause of them.

Alcohol addiction makes it tough to develop fresh solutions because they’re fixated on one particular issue.

Did you know? According to Nova Rehab, one of the world’s leading rehabilitation centers, each year, 88,000 Americans die from alcohol-related causes in the United States, placing it third after cigarettes and poor diet as a significant cause of preventable mortality.

chapters.title: The problem with addiction is that we seldom maintain boundaries when it comes to consuming things we like
chapters.text:
Rich Roll had always been aware of the type of guy he was, which is why he had never experimented with heavy drugs. He knew that if he took cocaine or heroin, he would fall in love with it right away. He was easily swayed by anything that may lure him away from his shyness. We, like many others, are aware of this, but we are unconcerned. It’s why, despite knowing the dangers, we still battle obesity because we are unwilling to give up meals containing visceral fats.

When we get addicted to anything, our willpower is weakened, making it harder to quit what we are addicted to.

Unable to bear the shame of his lifestyle any longer, he sought the aid of a professional recommended by his manager at work. His original assumption was that he couldn’t join a group class to talk about his difficulties. He was wary about the types of individuals he’d encounter there. However, the group class was bright and alive with smiling professionals — men of all ages in suits and vivacious gorgeous ladies catching up on life, far from what he had imagined.

He responded when the group inquired who he was, and when they asked what he did, he said he was an alcoholic. It was the first time he’d said those things out loud. But it had an instantaneous and unexpectedly significant influence on his mind. He attempted to stay sober after that, but he had financial problems that took him back to drinking. As a result, he went back to drinking time after time again, often in a spectacular manner, not just where he left off, but often in a considerably worse position.

He continued to drink although it was no longer enjoyable, and it was then he understood how helpless he was against this beast. He was, nevertheless, resolute. So, when Michele entered the scene, he had a purpose of being sober other than himself, but it didn’t work out either. Rich Roll returned to excessive drinking after she began having an affair just weeks before their wedding.

chapters.title: Accepting that we can’t do it all on our own is sometimes the answer to our problems
chapters.text:
Rich Roll chose to consult a psychologist who his father had suggested. Dr. Garrett O’Connor, the psychologist, had a profound impact on his life. He urged Roll to handle his condition as if it were a disease and seek rehab — a minimum of 30 days away from his issues, with nothing to do but concentrate on them in a carefully controlled setting.

One of his first tasks at the recovery clinic was to write a thorough description of his top 10 most disastrous alcoholic misadventures. Then he was forced to read the paper in front of the whole staff and patients. This gave him a deep insight into the effects of drinking too much.

He had no intention of staying in treatment for a long time at the moment. His strategy was to focus on his thinking for a brief period. But he was made to understand that his career, not sobriety, was his primary concern. He had no future if he didn’t do it immediately and correctly. As a result, he decided to remain in rehab for as long as needed. And, over time, he began to open up about his problem, realizing that he had no control over most things in life. He also realized that his belief in his ability to regulate his drinking had left his life not only unmanageable but in shambles.

We can’t assist someone who doesn’t want help; people will be more eager to help us if we show that they can count on us.

Most importantly, he realized that being an alcoholic wasn’t entirely his fault. He had an illness and needed to follow a treatment plan. It’s only that at that time, there weren’t any medications available to treat alcoholism. The answer, therefore, was spiritual. This knowledge was reassuring, a revelation that he no longer had to be completely responsible for all his issues by making a simple choice. That wasn’t to suggest he relinquished complete control over his life, just that he became willing not just to seek aid but also to accept it.

chapters.title: Fear is the main cause of all our confusing emotions
chapters.text:
Fear of people, places, things, economic insecurity, the unknown, and occurrences that haven’t happened affects most of us. And there’s only one way to get rid of this dread — faith.

Bridging the fear-faith divide began with another assignment that Rich Roll felt driven to share with others. He was requested to recite his list of unethical activities. Through this, he was able to realize that failure isn’t always finite. He realized that something good might be found in negative situations.

In most cases, we don’t realize what a thing is or what it can do to us; we just go ahead and overuse it, which can lead to addiction.

After Rich Roll quit drinking, he turned his attention to nutrition. He chose to embark on a trial of what is known as a vegan or plant-based, whole-food diet. His diet was free of not just all animal products but also most processed items. He modified and changed the program in five years to improve his athletic performance, prevent illness and disease, and maintain maximum long-term well-being for himself and his family. The thought of starting this initially scared him, but he overcame the fear.

He called the diet the PlantPower Diet. Even in its most basic version, the PlantPower Diet can provide us with a lot of energy. We’ll feel a lot lighter, and our energy levels will skyrocket. Our ability to think clearly will improve, as will our strength and endurance.

Plants help the body function at its optimum and may improve our health in various ways that are quite useful to our bodies. Plants don’t have the same amount of chemicals and preservatives as processed meals. They are all natural, less expensive, and better to eat.

chapters.title: Natural foods are better for us as they are closest to their original condition and simple to prepare
chapters.text:
Most individuals find it challenging to determine which meals are healthy in today’s world. We’re constantly bombarded with contradictory advertising and marketing messages advising us on what to eat. Almost every product has a banner or motto explaining why processed foods are good for us. Then there are the supplements that are constantly promoted to us. Vitamin pills, protein powders, energy bars, weight-loss drinks, nutraceuticals, and cure-all smoothies have all been sold to us as healthy supplements. But, unfortunately, these supplements, in principle, do more damage than benefit.

The basic conclusion is that we must consume plants. There are several types of vegetables, fruits, grains, seeds, and legumes that are good protein sources. We also need to keep things interesting by sticking to the outside of the grocery store and avoiding places filled with processed and refined goods. Things with components we can’t pronounce or that aren’t present in nature should be avoided. When feasible, we can consume organic and locally farmed vegetables.

To prevent diabetes, sugar, which is present in most processed meals, should be avoided at all costs.

Once we’ve gotten used to the shift, we’ll discover that adopting the PlantPower method is easier than we anticipated. But it doesn’t have to be difficult or time-consuming to eat plants. Our plant-based meals should be as near to their natural condition as possible.

There’s nothing wrong with taking things slowly at first. However, we have to let go of the need to be immediately flawless. If avoiding fast food for the first several weeks is all we can manage, that’s all right. After that, we may experiment with more plant-based meals in our routine.

chapters.title: It’s preferable to take little steps toward a PlantPower Diet than to do nothing
chapters.text:
It’s important to stay away from foods that contain too much fat because they are detrimental to our health. This is why the most appealing and unhealthy animal products and processed meals must be removed from the fridge and storage. This is a critical point. Keeping these meals concealed in the house isn’t going to benefit us at all. After that, we may use natural milk such as coconut milk to substitute dairy. Small actions like these will aid in the adaptation of our thoughts and bodies.

We will acquire a greater hunger and craving for healthy foods as we begin to feel better. With the satisfaction of favorable results, we will want to increase the number of plant-based meals on our daily menu.

The goal of fitness is to promote sustainability by changing our flexible behaviors and altering the inflexible ones.

Although a goal must have personal significance, it is best served when it is also in service to something larger than oneself. This method can help us become more honest and give our search a purpose and meaning beyond self-interest.

It’s pointless to put unnecessary pressure on ourselves. Instead, we should freely and selflessly love ourselves. Patience is what gives purpose to our existence. It gives us a higher feeling of purpose.

There’s also no need to start inquiring about all the equipment we’ll need to get started on being fit. Although determining what works best for us is critical, we must recognize that it is unnecessary to question why we are not in the finest form possible. In the vast majority of situations, these inquiries only make us feel helpless in the long run.

It is not necessary to be frightened of rehab. However, we must recognize that our fear of rehab is a natural response to stepping outside our comfort zone. We can overcome any addiction as long as we remain committed to finding a solution to our issues.

When purpose aligns with faith, there can be no failure and all needs will be met — because the universe is infinitely abundant. ~ Rich Roll

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
Addiction is widespread in our society because we let emotions dictate behavior. When this occurs, we are prone to mistaking brief relief for simple answers. We turn to alcohol, drugs, cigarettes, or social media rather than confronting the issue. We don’t want to deal with the problems we’re facing, so we act as if they don’t exist. Problems don’t go away just because we wish they weren’t there. We can seek assistance or tackle a problem in a variety of ways. For example, we can develop a mantra to keep us going or seek professional help. We must also be true to our feelings by not letting them stand in the way of doing the right thing.

Rich Roll’s life story tells us that setbacks are an inevitable part of nature, and if we wait until we are at our lowest point before acting, we might remain stuck. However, once we notice a negative trait, it’s best to accept our weakness and find help immediately.

Our objective should be to make steady improvement rather than perfection. It doesn’t matter if our 90-minute workout is cut to 60 minutes; we shouldn’t give up because it doesn’t fit our ideal timetable. Instead, try to do the task and get on with your day. It is preferable to undertake something in small bits rather than not at all.

Above all, it’s helpful to understand that failure is a sign of bravery. It implies we have the courage to put ourselves to the test and the boldness to go outside our comfort zones. We can always congratulate ourselves on our small achievements and acknowledge that we tried.

Try this

Take a day to open your fridge and take out every processed food. Replace every drink with water or naturally processed fruits, and remove everything that contains sugar.

Read it till the end?
